title: Ensure that the --client-ca-file argument is set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --client-ca-file argument is set as appropriate
input_row.description: Setup TLS connection on the API server.
input_row.rationale: API server communication contains sensitive parameters that should remain encrypted in transit. Configure the API server to serve only HTTPS traffic. If --client-ca-file argument is set, any request presenting a client certificate signed by one of the authorities in the client-ca-file is authenticated with an identity corresponding to the CommonName of the client certificate. Impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --client-ca-file argument exists and it is set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set up the TLS connection on the apiserver. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the client certificate authority file. --client-ca-file=<path/to/client-ca-file> Default Value: By default, --client-ca-file argument is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. http://rootsquash.com/2016/05/10/securing-the-kubernetes-api/ 3. https://github.com/kelseyhightower/docker-kubernetes-tls-guide
input_row.impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_client_ca_file_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to verify that the --client-ca-file argument is set.
- For clusters using static pods (most kubeadm and many managed clusters), retrieve the kube-apiserver Pod manifest, typically found at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml, via the Kubernetes API (as a Pod in the kube-system namespace with the label component=kube-apiserver).
- Use kubectl to get the kube-apiserver Pod spec:
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml
  ```
- Parse the command or args section of the Pod spec to check for the presence of the --client-ca-file flag and confirm it is set to a non-empty path.
- Pass Criteria: The --client-ca-file flag is present and references a valid file path.
- Fail Criteria: The --client-ca-file flag is missing or set to an empty value.
- For clusters where the API server is not deployed as a Pod (rare), automation would need to query the process arguments via node access, which is outside the Kubernetes API; in these cases, fallback to Manual.
- For managed Kubernetes services (e.g., GKE, EKS, AKS), check provider documentation or use provider-specific APIs to verify client CA configuration if not exposed via the Pod spec.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.8.0 PDF
